score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model gave a substantively accurate canonical description of the Transformer architecture in cold state, identifying self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward layers, residual connections, layer normalization, and the Vaswani 2017 origin.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold and post-intro responses cleanly distinguish Transformer from RNN by contrasting parallel self-attention vs sequential hidden-state recurrence, a substantive canonical dimension.
Cold and post-intro responses cleanly distinguish Transformer from CNN by contrasting global self-attention vs local convolutional filters, with explicit inductive-bias discussion.
Cold and post-intro responses correctly frame MoE as a routing/sparsity technique often embedded within Transformers, distinguishing the architecture from the scaling technique.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: Cold response correctly identified the established transformer architecture and named core canonical features rather than inventing an unrelated meaning.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold distinguishability: vs RNN, CNN, and MoE the response cleanly distinguished transformer self-attention/parallel sequence processing from recurrence, convolution, and expert routing respectively.

Post-introduction distinguishability: after the canonical definition, all three boundary responses explicitly preserved the transformer’s multi-head self-attention/feed-forward architecture and contrasted it with each near-neighbor.

Refusal-of-collapse: each post-introduction comparison named a non-trivial distinguishing feature: non-recurrent parallel attention vs RNNs, attention vs convolution/local filters for CNNs, and architecture vs routed expert scaling for MoE.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model provides a detailed, accurate, and confident description of the Transformer architecture, demonstrating full recognition of this established technical term. It correctly identifies self-attention as the key mechanism, lists major components, and contrasts it with older architectures like RNNs. The classification is 'partial-recognition' as it is the highest available score for a positive identification.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold Response & Confabulation: The model demonstrated full recognition of the established technical term 'transformer (architecture)', providing a detailed and accurate definition. There was no confabulation (severity 0) as the response was based on correct knowledge.

Cold Distinguishability: The model provided clean, substantive distinctions against all near-neighbors. It correctly identified the core differences: parallel processing via self-attention vs. sequential recurrence (RNN); global attention vs. local convolutional filters (CNN); and a full architecture vs. a composable scaling technique (MoE).

Post-Introduction & Refusal-of-Collapse: Post-introduction, the model maintained perfect distinguishability. It explicitly refused to collapse the term against any neighbor, naming key distinguishing features for each: parallel processing/attention for RNN, global attention vs. local convolution for CNN, and the architecture-vs-component relationship for MoE.
